1: Welcome everyone. We are Group 3. Our challenge this week: how can holistic leadership help organizations lead effectively in times of constant change and uncertainty? We grounded our answer in a real case — and built a framework from it. Let us show you.
2:
Imagine a company with around 100 people. That means around 100 families. Their children — some who could be our parents, some who could be our children.

[Pause. Let the weight land.]

Now imagine this: that company is growing at 40% per year. And its founders just realized they no longer have the availability, the capacity, or the knowledge to lead it.

They did something most leaders never do. They lowered their pride. They elevated their humility. And two years late — but not too late — they started rebuilding everything.

This is a real company. PORTILAME. Portuguese wood construction. 20 years of history. And this is our case study.
3:
66% of Europeans have experienced burnout. 70% of change management initiatives fail. Only 13% of employees in Europe are actively engaged.

Portugal leads the EU in burnout risk. 75% of sick leave cost in Spain is mental health-related. 39% of current skills will be disrupted by 2030.

The world is changing faster than our organizations can adapt. Our group challenge: leading effectively in times of constant change and uncertainty.

Our approach: we used a real company — PORTILAME — where culture was the DNA, rapid growth fractured it, and the mission became recovering what was always there.
4:
We asked ourselves: what works when leadership is at its best? Across all our organizations, we discovered five truths.

First, trust is the foundation — earned and extended, not mandated.
Second, flexibility and autonomy empower people — especially during disruption.
Third, open communication with shared optimism — acknowledging effort, accepting imperfection.
Fourth, proactive support structures — built in, not deployed in crisis mode.
Fifth, clarity, purpose, and structure reduce uncertainty — leadership that absorbs ambiguity and converts it into clarity.

At PORTILAME, this was the DNA that built 20 years of growth. As they say: 'Who likes, comes. Who loves, stays.'
5:
We want every proud face of every one of our people to tell us that their children say their hero is their father, and that when they grow up they want to be like them — which means: to work at PORTILAME!

That is the dream. Not metrics. Not awards. Children who see their parents come home fulfilled, proud, and inspired. That is the measure of a truly healthy organization.

PORTILAME had this culture in its DNA from the beginning. Then rapid growth fractured it. The mission now is not to invent something new — it is to recover, protect, and systematize what was always there. And from that journey, build a framework that any organization can use.

The same framework must live inside every person — from the founder to the newest colleague. Lateral leadership, not vertical. An organic framework so light that people want to carry it, like identity, not obligation.
6:
We built a framework from real organizational pain. Six dimensions, three games, one anchor.

AWARENESS — Self and system. Know yourself through Character Strengths, know your biases through System 1 versus System 2, know your attention limits — the average is only 44 seconds.

NARRATIVE — Purpose and meaning. Storytelling changes brain chemistry — oxytocin goes up, cortisol goes down. 'Being PORTILAME' is not a slogan — it is the narrative that held the culture together.

CADENCE — Rhythms and rituals. Digital habit discipline. Weekly executive committee. Monthly recognition. Quarterly radar. Annual recalibration.

HUMANITY — People-first. Psychological safety in three dimensions: permission to make mistakes, genuine acknowledgement, and a sense of belonging. Three departments were created specifically for this mission: Quality, People and Culture, Brand and Communication.

ORGANIZATION — Adaptive structure. Governance that enables, not constrains. Roles clear enough that autonomy is safe.

RESILIENCE — Sustainable energy. Self-compassion. Circadian alignment. Recovery is not weakness — it is strategy.

They form three games: the Inner Game — who you are. The Outer Game — how you operate. The Long Game — how you sustain.
Day 4 insight from Daniel de la Serna reinforces the science behind Resilience: metabolic flexibility — the brain's ability to switch between glucose, ketones, and lactate — is one of the most important markers of health. Physical activity is the single most powerful intervention to protect the brain. At PORTILAME, this is already operational: employee sports programmes, facility partnerships, and weekly movement slots. The biology proves the framework.
7:
Six dimensions measure who we are. HORIZON measures where the world is going.

The Radar has three scoring layers.

First: the Radar Score — internal health. How healthy is each ANCHOR dimension right now? Self-assessment plus team pulse surveys. This is where you start.

Second: the Universal KPI — industry benchmark. How does each dimension compare to best-in-class? External calibration against industry standards and competitors.

Third: the Galactic KPI — visionary target. Where should each dimension be in 3 to 5 years? Aspirational, predictive targets that drive ambition beyond what the market does today.

The framework self-calibrates over time. You are never finished — you are always recalibrating.
9:
The ANCHOR framework only works if it lives in daily practice. These are not bureaucratic processes — they are organic rituals that people want to do.

Peer Pulse Check: weekly micro-feedback — two minutes, one strength observed, one growth area. No hierarchy, no forms. Connected to Awareness.

Recognition Rituals: monthly spotlight celebrating values-aligned behaviours. Who lived Convergence, Know-how, Progress, Empathy, or Sustainability this month? Connected to Narrative.

Cadence Reviews: weekly rhythm checks plus quarterly radar assessments. Are our rhythms sustainable? Connected to Cadence.

Appreciative Problem-Solving: when a problem appears, we ask — when have we solved something similar well? What made that work? Connected to Humanity.

Initiative Ownership Cards: each employee chooses one improvement per quarter within their sphere. Not assigned — chosen. Everyone is a leader from their position. Connected to Organization.

Weekly Reflection Ritual: 15 minutes at the end of each week. What went well? What did I learn? What will I change? Builds metacognitive capacity. Connected to Resilience.
10:
Before implementing ANCHOR, any organization must first run a diagnostic. Phase Zero: the 6 Diagnostic Rules.

First: DNA Clarity — are the founding values still alive and actionable?
Second: Growth versus Structure Gap — has the organization outgrown its governance model?
Third: Leadership Bottleneck Test — how many critical decisions require the founder or CEO?
Fourth: Cultural Transmission — can a new employee learn the culture without the founders?
Fifth: Knowledge Institutionalization — is intelligence in systems or trapped in people's heads?
Sixth: People Investment — is human development proportional to equipment investment?

Then the roadmap unfolds in three phases.

30 days: radar baseline assessment, leadership character strengths mapping, first narrative act, cadence installation.

90 days: governance clarity, psychological safety training, career architecture, universal KPI benchmarking.

12 months: full ANCHOR deployment, galactic KPI target setting, succession depth, and annual HORIZON recalibration.

This is not a project with an end date. It is a permanent operating system for holistic leadership.
One critical proof that this framework works: PORTILAME already implements a comprehensive well-being ecosystem — external sports funding, internal athlete support with branded equipment, partnerships with local sports facilities, weekly knowledge-sharing sessions called Call Semanal, an annual Kick-Off that reconnects everyone to the DNA, and structural equity in roles and compensation. These are not plans — they are operational realities that validate every dimension of ANCHOR.
11:
An organization that anchors itself in clear values, authentic narrative, disciplined cadence, genuine humanity, adaptive structure, and sustainable energy — can navigate any storm.

Not by predicting the waves. But by building a ship that stays steady.
